questions.question: Why is money considered a medium of exchange?

options.B: It eliminates double coincidence of wants

questions.explanation: Money removes the need for double coincidence of wants in barter.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of money?
questions.explanation: Money does not produce goods; it facilitates exchange.

questions.question: Why do banks ask for collateral while giving loans?
questions.explanation: Collateral acts as security if the borrower fails to repay.

questions.question: Which situation represents productive credit?

options.C: Loan for setting up a small business

questions.explanation: Productive credit generates income.

questions.question: Why is informal credit considered risky?

options.C: High interest rates and no regulation

questions.explanation: Informal lenders often charge high interest without regulation.

questions.question: Demand deposits are important because they:
questions.explanation: Demand deposits can be used through cheques and act as money.

questions.question: Which of the following is regulated by RBI?
questions.explanation: Commercial banks are regulated by RBI.

questions.question: What is the main objective of Self Help Groups?
questions.explanation: SHGs provide small loans to members.

questions.question: Which example shows a debt trap?

options.C: Borrowing repeatedly to repay old loans

questions.explanation: Debt trap occurs when borrower cannot repay and borrows again.

questions.question: Which of the following improves access to formal credit?
questions.explanation: More bank branches improve credit access.

questions.question: Why is bank credit considered cheaper than moneylender credit?
questions.explanation: Banks generally charge lower interest.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of formal credit?
questions.explanation: Cooperative banks are part of formal sector.

questions.question: Why is credit important for farmers?
questions.explanation: Farmers need credit for agricultural inputs.

questions.question: Which of the following reflects legal tender?
questions.explanation: RBI issued currency is legal tender.

questions.question: What happens if borrower fails to repay a secured loan?
questions.explanation: Collateral is seized if loan is unpaid.

questions.question: Which form of money is easiest for online shopping?
questions.explanation: Digital payments are used for online transactions.

questions.question: Which credit situation can lead to poverty?
questions.explanation: High interest loans can trap borrowers in debt.

questions.question: Why are banks able to give loans?
questions.explanation: Banks lend money from deposits collected.

questions.question: Which function of money helps in saving for future?
questions.explanation: Money stores value for future use.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of formal credit?
questions.explanation: Formal credit provides legal protection.

questions.question: Which of the following increases financial inclusion?
questions.explanation: Jan Dhan Yojana promotes bank account access.

questions.question: Why is documentation required for bank loans?
questions.explanation: Documentation ensures accountability.

questions.question: Which situation reflects measure of value function?
questions.explanation: Money helps compare values of goods.

questions.question: Why is saving important in banking?
questions.explanation: Savings provide funds for bank lending.

questions.question: Which of the following is safest for long-term borrowing?
questions.explanation: Formal bank loans are regulated and safer.
